purpose: Define planning-adjacent capability boundaries before any future package extraction work.

surface_value.discovery: Use the active #465 execplan or package tests; ordinary agents should not read this during startup.
surface_value.why_it_deserves_to_exist: It prevents planning from absorbing adjacent capabilities by recording extraction criteria and blockers in one testable package-local artifact.

extraction_criteria: Repeated dogfooding pressure is hard to solve cleanly inside current modules or the workspace layer.
The contract shape is stable and no longer depends on sibling internals or informal conventions.
Ownership and boundaries are clear enough for selective adoption.
A repository can benefit from the capability without adopting the whole current planning stack.
Extraction would reduce total operating cost rather than create another surface agents must remember.

decisions.capability: Review / audit lane plus review-finding promotion
decisions.current_home: Optional planning payload and bundled planning review skills
decisions.candidate_boundary: A provider-agnostic review package could own review modes, review record schemas, finding caps, promotion thresholds, and review-to-follow-up routing.
decisions.why_not_core_planning: Daily execution planning only needs active state, next action, proof, and archive discipline; review portfolios are useful but separable quality and improvement workflows.
evidence_required_before_extraction: Review records are reused across distinct repo shapes without planning-specific assumptions.
Finding promotion can target provider-agnostic external work evidence or checked-in planning without assuming GitHub.
The review schema stays stable across at least one more compression or audit lane.
blockers: Review skills and docs still refer to planning-specific closeout and roadmap surfaces.
Promotion behavior is not yet proven as a standalone lifecycle.

decisions.capability: External work intake, external intent evidence, and reconciliation
decisions.current_home: Planning state, external-intent evidence contract, reconcile output, and optional intake skill
decisions.candidate_boundary: A tracker-agnostic intake package could own external work evidence schemas, intake/refinement rules, stale-state reconciliation, and provider adapter boundaries.
decisions.why_not_core_planning: Core planning should preserve active execution state without assuming any external tracker; intake and reconciliation are valuable only for repos with external work systems.
evidence_required_before_extraction: The provider-agnostic evidence schema supports at least one non-GitHub adapter or a documented adapter fixture.
Intake can create or update checked-in planning state without embedding provider-specific semantics.
Reconciliation proves useful outside long-running dogfooding lanes.
blockers: The only live adapter is currently GitHub CLI based.
Intake and reconciliation still depend on planning state shape for closeout and continuation decisions.

decisions.capability: Autopilot, bounded delegated judgment, orchestration, and capability-aware execution
decisions.current_home: Optional planning docs, planning skills, mixed-agent config reporting, and workspace defaults
decisions.candidate_boundary: A future operation package could own capability-fit rules, delegated judgment packets, autopilot loop semantics, and orchestration handoffs.
decisions.why_not_core_planning: These workflows can be high leverage, but they depend heavily on agent/runtime availability and should not burden the smallest valid planning install.
evidence_required_before_extraction: Capability and delegation records can stay repo-, agent-, tool-, and language-agnostic.
The workflow improves total operating cost across multiple agent runtimes.
Closeout trust and escalation rules stabilize without requiring planning internals.
blockers: Current dogfooding evidence is mostly from this repo and this agent workflow.
Delegation and autopilot wording still needs compression before becoming product-general.

decisions.capability: Reporting, signal hygiene, standing intent, and knowledge-promotion workflow
decisions.current_home: Optional planning docs, workspace reports, and memory/planning coordination
decisions.candidate_boundary: A future reporting or signal package would own compact report sections, signal routing, and durable-knowledge promotion contracts across modules.
decisions.why_not_core_planning: Core planning should expose compact active execution state; high-volume reporting and promotion policy should stay behind selectors or module-specific outputs.
evidence_required_before_extraction: Multiple modules need the same report-section schemas and signal-routing rules.
Knowledge promotion works with and without Agentic Memory installed.
Default report compactness can be preserved while selectors expose richer detail.
blockers: Reporting still mixes workspace aggregation, planning state, and memory coordination.
The feature-tier lane has not yet decided how much advanced reporting belongs in optional tiers.

decisions.capability: Generated-surface trust, maintainer proof, and package helper mirrors
decisions.current_home: Package-local generated adapters, render/check helpers, proof selectors, and maintainer tests
decisions.candidate_boundary: Do not extract as a standalone planning-adjacent package now; route generic command-generation work through the existing command-generation extraction lane.
decisions.why_not_core_planning: These surfaces help maintain the product but should not ship as ordinary target-repo operating input.
evidence_required_before_extraction: A non-planning generated package needs the same provenance and trust contract.
The command-generation extraction lane proves a reusable generator boundary.
Generated-surface trust can be expressed without planning payload assumptions.
blockers: Current helper mirrors are package-maintainer artifacts, not user-facing capability surfaces.
The broader command-generation extraction boundary is still open.

follow_up_policy.reason: Existing lanes already cover feature tiers, upstream signal capture, and command-generation extraction; this slice should not create duplicate follow-up issues until #466 refreshes the capability map.

next_step: Use #466 to refresh docs/agent-os-capabilities.md against these current module boundaries without turning the doctrine page into a backlog.
